title: To nullify the Presidential memoranda on the withdrawal of certain areas of the outer Continental Shelf from oil or natural gas leasing.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.
plain_summary: This bill cancels two orders that President Biden signed in January 2025 that banned oil and gas drilling in certain ocean areas. The banned areas included parts of the Gulf of Mexico, Atlantic and Pacific coasts, and the Northern Bering Sea near Alaska. If this bill passes, oil and gas companies would be allowed to get permits to drill in these ocean areas again. The bill basically undoes the President's decision to protect these waters from oil and gas drilling.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1 Nullification of Presidential Memoranda on the Withdrawal of Certain Areas of the Outer Continental Shelf
sections.text: The following Presidential memoranda (official written orders from the President) will have no power and will not be enforced. First, the Presidential Memorandum from January sixth, two thousand twenty five, titled Memorandum on the Withdrawal of Certain Areas of the United States Outer Continental Shelf from Oil or Natural Gas Leasing. This memorandum deals with areas in the Gulf of Mexico, Atlantic Ocean, and Pacific Ocean. Second, the Presidential Memorandum from January sixth, two thousand twenty five, titled Memorandum on the Withdrawal of Certain Areas of the United States Outer Continental Shelf from Oil or Natural Gas Leasing. This memorandum deals with the Northern Bering Sea Climate Resilience Area.
